name: Json.InElm

unions.name: JProp
unions.comment:
A `JProp` represents the type of a scalar property within a JSON document. These are regarded separately
from the `JValue` type for a few important reasons:

  - Since this package is concerned with parsing an arbitrary JSON document and lifting it into a type that allows it to be
    used within an Elm program, this means that the JSON schema is derived ad-hoc from user input.
  - And so, an ad-hoc JSON schema cannot exist without values.
  - However, if we are going to to store references to locations within that ad-hoc JSON schema, we are likely doing so because
    we would like to retrieve some value at a given location from a JSON document that is different from the original input.

In order to create a distinction between "a parsed JSON value" and "a reference to a location within a JSON document", it must
be the case that we have another way to represent the concept of "the expected type of a value at a location within a JSON document"
that is separate from "a value at a location within a JSON document, from which the type is derived".

Creating a second type was the most straightforward way to implement this behavior.

unions.name: JValue
unions.comment: A `JValue` represents a typed value within a JSON document.

aliases.name: Keypath
aliases.comment:
A `Keypath` is a data structure that stores the JSON path to a value of a given key.

Elements in a `Keypath` are either the name of a field, or the index of an array.

Use the functions in the `Json.InElm.Keypath` module to work with `Keypath` values.

aliases.name: Node
aliases.comment:
A `Node` is a reprentation of part of a JSON document, along with everything that is inside of it,
along with its "key path", which is its location within a JSON document.

This means that the output of the parser will always be a single `Node`; but depending on the input,
that `Node`'s `JValue` may have other `Node`s inside of it.
aliases.type: { value : Json.InElm.JValue, keypath : Json.InElm.Keypath, jProp : Maybe.Maybe Json.InElm.JProp }

values.name: parseJsonString
values.comment: Parse a raw JSON string to a `Node`.

values.name: parseValueAt
values.comment: Parse a `Json.Decode.Value` to a `Node`, at a given `KeyPath`.

values.name: toBool
values.comment: Try to get a `Bool` from a `JValue`.

values.name: toDecoder
values.comment: Create a decoder to a `Node`, given a `Keypath`.

values.name: toFloat
values.comment: Try to get a `Float` from a `JValue`.

values.name: toInt
values.comment: Try to get a `Int` from a `JValue`.

values.name: toList
values.comment: Try to get a `List Node` from a `JValue`.

values.name: toNull
values.comment: Try to see if a `JValue` contains a JSON `null`.

values.name: toObject
values.comment:
Try to get a `List (String, Node)` from a `JValue`, where the `String`
value is the name of the field in the object.

values.name: toString
values.comment: Try to get a `String` from a `JValue`.

values.name: toTime
values.comment: Try to get a `Time` from a `JValue`.

values.name: view
values.comment:
Combinator that allows you to build a view function that renders a `Node`; i.e., if _you_ can provide all of the functions
to destructure a `Node`, we can display it for you!
values.type: { string : String.String -> Html.Html msg, int : Basics.Int -> Html.Html msg, float : Basics.Float -> Html.Html msg, bool : Basics.Bool -> Html.Html msg, time : Time.Posix -> Html.Html msg, null : Html.Html msg, list : List.List (Html.Html msg) -> Html.Html msg, object : { key : String.String, value : Html.Html msg } -> Html.Html msg, withKeypath : Maybe.Maybe (String.String -> Html.Html msg -> Html.Html msg), withOnClick : Maybe.Maybe (Json.InElm.Node -> msg) } -> Json.InElm.Node -> Html.Html msg

name: Json.InElm.Keypath

aliases.name: FieldAccessor
aliases.comment: Representation of a value used to access a field in a JSON document.

values.name: at
values.comment: Updates a `KeyPath` and adds the name of a field in a JSON document.

values.name: fold
values.comment:
Fold over the values in a `KeyPath` to transform them into a value of some other type.

(Hint: `toString` implements `fold` directly! You can check out the source on Github to see how it's done.)
values.type: (a -> b -> b) -> { fromIndex : Basics.Int -> a, fromAt : String.String -> a } -> b -> Json.InElm.Keypath.Keypath -> b

values.name: index
values.comment: Updates a `KeyPath` and adds the value of an index in a JSON array.

values.name: init
values.comment: To construct a `Keypath`, you must start with an empty value.
